disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe colicky lower abdominal pain in waves, coming on ~4 hours ago after a heavy rich meal, followed by explosive watery stool
trigger_question_examples: When did this start and what were you doing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on a few hours ago after a big rich meal — gripping cramps low down that come in waves, and then I pass explosive watery stool.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Passing abundant watery stool and flatus — no vomiting and no absolute constipation
trigger_question_examples: Are you still passing stool or wind?
Have you been vomiting at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm passing lots of watery stool and wind — no vomiting at all, I don't even feel sick.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No blood or mucus visible in the stool
trigger_question_examples: Have you seen any blood or mucus in the toilet?
Is the stool just water, or is there red or black in it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blood, it's just water — no red or black, no slime.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Afebrile by home thermometer, no rigors or chills
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers, sweats or shakes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever — I checked and it's 36.9 on the thermometer, no shakes or chills.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No localised right-lower-quadrant pain; pain is diffuse across the lower abdomen and colicky, and appetite was normal beforehand
trigger_question_examples: Is the pain worse in one particular spot, like the lower right side?
Does it stay in one place or move around?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's spread across the whole lower belly and comes in waves — it isn't stuck in one spot like my right lower side.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known long-standing minor bowel condition — prior colonoscopy macroscopically normal, biopsies showed microscopic inflammation, told it is non-damaging
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any known bowel conditions?
Have you had any tests on your bowel before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've got a minor bowel thing — a camera test looked normal but the biopsies showed some inflammation cells; they said it isn't serious.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Intense health anxiety — patient fears bowel obstruction or rupture and is seeking reassurance
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried this could be?
How are you coping with the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm scared my bowel is blocked or about to burst — the pain feels that bad.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Abdominal wall tenderness, guarding, rigidity, rebound, distension and bowel sounds cannot be assessed in this encounter (no clinician to palpate/auscultate)
trigger_question_examples: Is your abdomen hard or swollen when you press on it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can push on my own tummy a bit but I can't really tell you if it's guarding or rebound — you can't feel it through the screen.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm not sure how to check that properly myself.
